local_names.romanized_local: Lì zhī bǎi xiāng guǒ hóng chá (zhōng bēi)

romanized: Lì zhī bǎi xiāng guǒ hóng chá (zhōng bēi)

food_subgroup: Fruit-flavoured black tea; sweetened ready-to-drink tea beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general black tea beverage class (FOODON_03311171). No specific lychee-passion-fruit flavoured black tea entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers liquid black tea as a category. No flavoured variant code exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 covers black tea as a substance. The flavoured, sweetened beverage variant is not separately coded; closest_parent used.

serving.description: 1 medium cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g as served)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml, as typically served at KOI Thé and similar Taiwanese tea chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sweetened black tea with fruit flavouring — estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355 (Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water) and USDA Branded / SR Legacy sugar-sweetened tea analogues; lychee and passion fruit syrup contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central fruit syrup entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for lychee-passion-fruit black tea exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central plain brewed black tea (NDB 14355, ~1 kcal/100 g, negligible macros) with a typical sweetened fruit-flavoured tea profile. KOI Thé medium-cup sugar content is publicly estimated at approximately 25–35 g sugar per 500 ml serving based on industry-standard Taiwanese tea chain formulations; per-100 g values are derived accordingly. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Fructose-sweetened and sucrose-sweetened beverages — GI analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea and fruit-flavoured beverage data; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lychee-passion-fruit black tea. GI estimated at 55 based on sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured tea analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al.). Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 32.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 17.9, rounded to 18.2 accounting for fructose contribution from fruit syrups. gi_category set to low.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~25 mg per 500 ml serving); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is negligible at approximately 25 mg per medium serving, posing minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, regular consumption of sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain, which is an indirect hypertension risk factor.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar load (~31.5 g per 500 ml serving) from lychee and passion fruit syrups causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this beverage due to its high free sugar content (approximately 31.5 g per medium serving), which can cause significant postprandial hyperglycaemia; requesting sugar-free or reduced-sugar options is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains no fat or cholesterol, its high free sugar content (~31.5 g per serving) is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk when consumed habitually; limiting frequency of consumption is recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Passion Fruit Black Tea (Medium) is a popular Taiwanese-style fruit-flavoured black tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a well-known Taiwanese tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The drink is prepared by brewing black tea and combining it with lychee-flavoured syrup and passion fruit syrup, then sweetening with sugar or fructose syrup and serving chilled or over ice in a medium-sized cup of approximately 500 ml. The flavour profile is fragrant, floral, and tropical, with the delicate lychee sweetness complemented by the tangy, aromatic notes of passion fruit, all underpinned by the mild astringency of black tea. The beverage is dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences. Nutritionally, it is a sweetened beverage with approximately 26 kcal per 100 ml and around 130 kcal per medium serving, derived almost entirely from added sugars (approximately 31.5 g per serving). Protein and fat content are effectively zero. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), and the glycemic load per serving is substantial at approximately 18, making it a concern for individuals managing blood glucose levels, including those with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is very low. The beverage contains trace amounts of potassium and antioxidant compounds from black tea, including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with modest cardiovascular benefits in epidemiological studies, though these benefits are likely offset by the high sugar load in this sweetened formulation. Clinically, habitual consumption of high-sugar beverages such as this is associated with increased risk of obesity, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Consumers are advised to request reduced-sugar or sugar-free versions where available.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for lychee-passion-fruit black tea exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central plain brewed black tea data with industry-standard Taiwanese tea chain sweetened beverage profiles. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero as this is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central plain black tea and fruit syrup analogues. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, and cholesterol_mg are all naturally zero for this beverage type. 2. glycemic_index set to 55 (low) based on analogous sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured tea estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables; no direct GI measurement available for this specific product. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries used for estimation and are not expected to be present in meaningful quantities in this beverage. 4. Serving size of 500 g is used as the standard medium cup volume for KOI Thé Singapore. 5. Sugar content per serving (~31.5 g) is estimated from industry-standard Taiwanese tea chain formulations; actual sugar content varies with customisation level (sugar level options: 0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%). Values reported here correspond to the default 100% sugar level. 6. The input description '中-荔枝百香红茶' uses '中' to denote medium cup size; this is preserved in aliases.

Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `notes` fields to reflect this change. Verified aliases_by_language consistency and all -1 sentinel values documentation.
